saints.name: Translation of the relics of the Hieromartyr Ignatius, the Godbearer and Bishop of Antioch

troparion.text: Like a treasure of rich and abundant gifts  /  your relics were carried reverently from Rome to your flock.  / While lovingly celebrating their return,  /  we receive the grace of healing for our souls and bodies:  /  and we always sing of your martyric contest, O glorious Hieromartyr Ignatius.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You appeared today…”)
Today you shone forth from the East,  /  enlightening all of creation with your teachings,  /  and you were adorned with martyrdom, O godly Ignatius the God-bearer.

saints.name: Saint Laurence, Recluse of the Kiev Caves, Far Caves, and Bishop of Turov

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Laurence, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Laurence, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Gerasimus, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Gerasimus, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Pitirim, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Pitirim, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Jonah, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Jonah, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

saints.name: Martyrs Romanus, Jacob (James), Philotheus, Hyperechius, Abibus, Julian, and Paregonius, at Samosata

troparion.text: We implore You, O Lord, for the sake of the sufferings of Your Saints,  / which they endured for Your sake,  /  show compassion unto us, and heal all our infirmities,  / O Lover of mankind.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O divine Martyrs, you appeared as bright lamps,  /  enlightening all of creation with the radiance of your miracles,  / unceasingly supplicating Christ God on behalf of us all,  / thereby releasing us from our maladies,  /  and ever driving away the most profound darkness of ignorance.

saints.name: Martyr Ashot Curapalati, King of Artanuji

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Ashot, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Ashot;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

saints.name: Saint Ignatius, Wonderworker and Bishop of Smolensk

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Ignatius, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Ignatius, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

saints.name: Martyrs Sarbelus and Bebaia of Edessa

troparion.text: Your holy martyrs O Lord, / through their sufferings have received incorruptible crowns from You, our God. / For having Your strength, they laid low their adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through their intercessions, save our souls!

Your holy martyr Demetrius, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Demetrius;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Akepsimas, O Lord, / through his sufferings has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Akepsimas;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.
